description: The daemon status check (used by ao daemon status and the MCP ao.daemon.status tool) currently reports 'running' based on state file presence, not actual process liveness. Write the daemon PID to a pid file at ~/.ao/<scope>/daemon/daemon.pid on startup. In the status handler, verify the PID is alive (kill(pid, 0) on unix). If the process is dead but state says running, report 'dead' or 'crashed' status and suggest restarting. Also update the MCP health tool to include process_alive field.
title: Fix daemon status to verify PID liveness instead of relying on stale state
